item.title: Will the Pentagon’s Anthropic controversy scare startups away from defense work?
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: On the latest episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, we discussed what the controversy means for other startups seeking to work with the federal government.

item.title: Owner of ICE detention facility sees big opportunity in AI man camps
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: AI data center developers are increasingly relying on a style of camp popularized as housing for men working in remote oil fields.

item.title: A roadmap for AI, if anyone will listen
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: The Pro-Human Declaration was finalized before last week's Pentagon-Anthropic standoff, but the collision of the two events wasn’t lost on anyone involved.

item.title: Google just gave Sundar Pichai a $692M pay package
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Most of it is tied to performance, including new stock incentives linked to Waymo and Wing, its drone delivery venture.

item.title: Grammarly’s ‘expert review’ is just missing the actual experts
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: A recently-added feature in Grammarly purports to improve users’ writing with help from the world's great writers and thinkers — and some tech journalists, too.

item.title: OpenAI hardware exec Caitlin Kalinowski quits in response to Pentagon deal
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Hardware executive Caitlin Kalinowski announced today that in response to OpenAI's controversial agreement with the Department of Defense, she’s resigned from her role leading the company's robotics team.

item.title: OpenAI delays ChatGPT’s ‘adult mode’ again
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The feature, which will give verified adult users access to erotica and other adult content, had already been delayed from December.

item.title: Microsoft, Google, Amazon say Anthropic Claude remains available to non-defense customers
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort, Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Trump's Department of War feud with Anthropic won't impact other companies that are using Claude via Microsoft and Google products.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude found 22 vulnerabilities in Firefox over two weeks
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: In a recent security partnership with Mozilla, Anthropic found 22 separate vulnerabilities in Firefox — 14 of them classified as "high-severity."

item.title: Anthropic’s Pentagon deal is a cautionary tale for startups chasing federal contracts
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: The Pentagon has officially designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk after the two failed to agree on how much control the military should have over its AI models, including its use in autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance. As Anthropic’s $200 million contract fell apart, the DoD turned to OpenAI instead, which accepted and then watched ChatGPT uninstalls surge 295%. As the stakes keep rising, the question remains: how much unrestricted […]

item.title: Anthropic vs. the Pentagon, the SaaSpocalypse, and why competitions is good, actually
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo, Kirsten Korosec, Anthony Ha, Sean O'Kane
item.description: The Pentagon has officially designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk after the two failed to agree on how much control the military should have over its AI models, including its use in autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance. As Anthropic’s $200 million contract fell apart, the DoD turned to OpenAI instead, which accepted and then watched ChatGPT uninstalls surge 295%. As the stakes keep rising, the question remains: how much unrestricted […]

item.title: Claude’s consumer growth surge continues after Pentagon deal debacle
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Claude's app is now seeing more new installs than ChatGPT and is growing its daily active users.

item.title: City Detect, which uses AI to help cities stay safe and clean, raises $13M Series A
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: City Detect, a company that helps local governments prevent urban decay, is in at least 17 cities so far, including Dallas and Miami.

item.title: After Europe, WhatsApp will let rival AI companies offer chatbots in Brazil
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Meta is now allowing rival AI companies to provide their chatbots on WhatsApp to Brazilian users for a fee, a day after the company confirmed a similar decision for users in Europe.

item.title: Anthropic to challenge DOD’s supply-chain label in court
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei said he plans to challenge the Department of Defense's designation of the AI firm as a supply-chain risk. He claims most Anthropic customers are unaffected by the label.

item.title: DiligenceSquared uses AI, voice agents to make M&A research affordable
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Instead of relying on expensive management consultants, the startup uses AI voice agents to conduct interviews with customers of the companies the PE firms are considering buying.

item.title: AWS launches a new AI agent platform specifically for healthcare
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: AWS is launching Amazon Connect Health, an AI agent platform that will help with patient scheduling, documentation, and patient verification.

item.title: US reportedly considering sweeping new chip export controls
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: In an alleged drafted proposal, the U.S. government would play a role in every chip export sale regardless of which country it's coming from.

item.title: It’s official: The Pentagon has labeled Anthropic a supply-chain risk
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: The Department of Defense has officially labeled Anthropic a supply-chain risk, making the AI firm the first American company with the label. Meanwhile, the DOD continues to use Anthropic's AI in Iran.

item.title: Luma launches creative AI agents powered by its new ‘Unified Intelligence’ models
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Luma introduced Luma Agents, powered by its new “Unified Intelligence” models, designed to coordinate multiple AI systems and generate end-to-end creative work across text, images, video and audio.
